framework.name: First-Party Fraud as Core Agentic Commerce Problem
framework.description: The shift from human-authorized to agent-authorized transactions creates fundamental ambiguity: Did the consumer truly authorize this? Was it a trusted agent or rogue bot? Did the agent make an error? Traditional fraud detection assumes human-in-loop; agent commerce breaks this model. Merchants and issuers will bear dispute costs unless new authorization mechanisms exist.
framework.raw_quote: Merchants and issuers are wondering about first party fraud. Was this transaction really authorized by a consumer? Did this actually come from a trusted agent or was it a rogue bot?
